look_at.text: Bob Johnson, senior partner. He's in his 50s, grey hair, wearing a dark suit with a burgundy tie. He looks tired but not unkind.

talk_to.text: Bob nods. 'Just sign it and you're all set. There should be a pen around here somewhere.'
Bob sips his coffee contentedly. 'Alright, alright. Let me get that agreement for you.'

effects.text: Bob hands you a thick document: THE SEPARATION AGREEMENT.

pick_up.text: I can't just pick up a lawyer. There are laws against that.

combinations.text: Bob's eyes light up. 'Oh, bless you! I've been running on fumes all day.'

effects.text: Bob takes a long sip and sighs contentedly.

combinations.text: Bob takes the coffee gratefully. 'You're a lifesaver!'
Bob glances at it. 'You can keep the badge. I need the separation agreement, not your visitor pass.'
'I have my own pens, thanks. But you might need that to sign the agreement.'
Bob takes the signed agreement and looks it over. 'Everything looks perfect. Congratulations, Alex. You're officially separated. I hope this is the start of something better for you.'
Bob reviews the signed agreement. 'All in order. You're free to go, Alex. Take care of yourself.'
'You haven't signed it yet! Find a pen and put your John Hancock on it first.'

look_at.text: A large oak desk covered in legal papers, a computer monitor, and a nameplate reading 'Bob Johnson, Esq.' There's also a half-empty coffee mug.

use.text: I shouldn't rummage through Bob's desk while he's sitting right there.

open.text: I can't open someone else's desk. That's rude. And possibly illegal.

look_at.text: Bob's computer. The screen shows what looks like a legal document. I can't read it from here.

use.text: I can't use Bob's computer. He's right there watching me.

look_at.text: A tall grey metal filing cabinet with four drawers. It looks like it holds decades of legal files.

open.text: I pull open the second drawer. It's full of files... and there's a nice pen rolling around in the back!

use.text: I should try to OPEN it.

look_at.text: A fancy ballpoint pen. Just what I need to sign the agreement.

pick_up.text: I grab the pen. This should do for signing the agreement.

use.text: I should PICK UP the pen first.

look_at.text: A large window overlooking the city. The view is nice — tall buildings, a park in the distance. At least something pleasant in this whole ordeal.

open.text: It's sealed shut. Probably for the best in a lawyer's office on the 15th floor.

use.text: I admire the view for a moment. Then I remember why I'm here.

look_at.text: Shelves lined with legal textbooks. 'Family Law in Practice', 'Divorce Proceedings 5th Edition', 'When Love Goes Wrong: A Legal Perspective'. Cheery stuff.

use.text: I'm not here to study law. I'm here to sign a piece of paper.

pull.text: This isn't a secret passage. It's a law office.

look_at.text: A fancy single-serve coffee machine sitting on a small table. It looks like it makes a decent cup.

use.text: I brew a fresh cup of coffee. The machine hums and gurgles. Perfect.
I could make some coffee, but I'm jittery enough as it is.

pick_up.text: The coffee machine is way too heavy and expensive to steal.

look_at.text: The door back to the lobby.

open.text: I head back to the lobby.

use.text: I go through the door to the lobby.
